 {"id":"wa-1u90p.2.7","title":"Implement input-first scheduling guardrails so typing/paste never starve during resize storms","description":"## Context\nUsers perceive resize quality primarily through interaction continuity. If typing/paste/mouse feels blocked, the system is unusable even if final frames are correct.\n\n## Scope\nAdd scheduling and fairness policies that reserve budget for interactive input processing while resize/reflow tasks are in flight.\n\n## Deliverables\n- Input-priority budget class in resize scheduler\n- Starvation-prevention policy and fairness accounting\n- Telemetry counters for input delay under resize load\n\n## Why this matters\nDirectly protects user trust and perceived responsiveness under worst-case workloads.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Input events remain within defined latency budget during synthetic resize storms.\n- Scheduler logs include per-frame budget split and deferred work reasons.\n- Unit and integration tests validate no starvation across competing work classes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:29.746292Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:51.479908Z","labels":["input-latency","reflow","resize","scheduler","track-control-plane","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2.7","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:29.746292Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2.7","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:38.262145Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2.7","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:38.367416Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.2.7","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:38.469227Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1u90p.3","title":"Layout/Reflow Engine: Logical Lines, Incremental Rewrap, Smart Breaks","description":"Refactor scrollback/layout to viewport-first incremental reflow and high-quality monospace-aware line breaking.

## Success Criteria
- Child tasks for this track are completed or explicitly deferred with rationale.
- Track-level objectives are met with measurable evidence (correctness + performance where applicable).
- Validation artifacts and operational notes are captured for future maintainers.","design":"Incremental reflow + smart wrapping","acceptance_criteria":"Layout correctness and bounded runtime proven","notes":"Knuth-Plass adaptation must be budget-safe","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.375651Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:29:43.57222Z","labels":["algorithms","epic","knuth-plass","reflow","resize","track-layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:24:22.375651Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:27.638237Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":47,"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"# Track 3 Detailed Intent: Layout/Reflow Algorithmic Core

## Core requirement
Decouple logical text from physical wraps and make reflow incremental.

## Smart wrapping objective
Use monospace-adapted Knuth-Plass for improved line quality while maintaining strict runtime bounds.

## Safety constraints
- bounded lookahead and deterministic fallback
- correctness preserved for Unicode width/cursor semantics
- allocation discipline in hot loops

## Why this matters
This is the heart of “looks great + feels instant.”
","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:28:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-1u90p.3.1","title":"Introduce logical-line canonical store plus width/font-keyed wrap cache","description":"Separate logical text representation from physical wrapped lines. Add cache invalidation rules keyed by width/font metrics so recompute work is bounded and predictable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":360,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.960581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:30:49.98208Z","labels":["cache","layout","reflow","resize","track-layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:44.960581Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.1","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:30.396047Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-1u90p.3.10","title":"[Subtask wa-1u90p.3.2] Prove cursor and logical-line consistency during progressive convergence","description":"## Scope\nGuarantee cursor position and logical-line mapping remain correct while reflow state is partially converged.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Property/integration tests validate cursor and mapping invariants across resize churn.\n- No visible semantic jumps in e2e scenarios with active typing during convergence.\n- Telemetry records consistency-check pass/fail stats.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.53728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.53728Z","labels":["correctness","cursor","reflow","resize","subtask","track-layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.10","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.53728Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-1u90p.3.11","title":"[Subtask wa-1u90p.3.3] Specify monospace Knuth-Plass cost model and bounded-complexity constraints","description":"## Scope\nDefine badness/penalty model, tie-break rules, and strict runtime bounds for terminal use.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Design spec includes complexity bound proof sketch and fallback trigger thresholds.\n- Unit tests validate cost monotonicity and tie-break determinism on fixed corpora.\n- Document maps terms to user-visible readability goals.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.649758Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.649758Z","labels":["algorithms","knuth-plass","reflow","resize","subtask","track-layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.11","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.649758Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-1u90p.3.12","title":"[Subtask wa-1u90p.3.3] Implement bounded DP engine with deterministic fallback path","description":"## Scope\nImplement DP-based line-break engine with lookahead cap and deterministic fallback for pathological tokens/lines.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Engine passes unit tests for correctness, determinism, and fallback behavior.\n- Performance tests verify bounded runtime under adversarial inputs.\n- Logs emit per-line mode (DP vs fallback) and timing metrics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.761596Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.761596Z","labels":["dp","knuth-plass","reflow","resize","subtask","track-layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.12","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.761596Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-1u90p.3.13","title":"[Subtask wa-1u90p.3.3] Integrate KP engine into reflow pipeline with readability regression gates","description":"## Scope\nWire KP engine into live reflow path and enforce quality regressions via wrap scorecard outputs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Integration tests prove correctness parity against baseline semantics.\n- Scorecard regressions are surfaced as machine-readable gate failures.\n- Configurable policy knobs are validated with defaults preserving performance targets.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.871556Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.871556Z","labels":["integration","knuth-plass","reflow","resize","subtask","track-layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.13","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T01:16:03.871556Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-1u90p.3.2","title":"Implement viewport-first incremental reflow with background cold-scrollback completion","description":"Prioritize visible and near-visible regions for immediate responsiveness, then complete remaining scrollback asynchronously. Maintain consistency guarantees during progressive completion.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":420,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:45.065547Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:30:50.081028Z","labels":["incremental","reflow","resize","track-layout","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:45.065547Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:30.502634Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:30.608727Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.3.4","title":"Optimize reflow hot path with scratch-buffer reuse and allocation minimization","description":"Eliminate avoidable allocations and repeated scans in core wrapping loops. Reuse per-worker scratch buffers to stabilize latency and reduce GC/allocator pressure.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.
- Integration and/or e2e coverage is updated where this task affects multi-component behavior.
- Structured telemetry/logging is added for observability and regression triage.
- No regressions in existing terminal semantics within the affected scope.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:45.277122Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:30:50.281801Z","labels":["allocation","optimization","reflow","resize","track-layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:25:45.277122Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:30.919462Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:26:31.026331Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.3.6","title":"Tune line-break readability cost model and expose policy/config controls","description":"Calibrate badness/penalty weights for terminal readability and predictable behavior. Provide configurable policy knobs with safe defaults and documented tradeoffs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is complete for the stated scope and behavior is validated.
- Unit tests are added/updated for core logic and edge cases.

 {"id":"wa-1u90p.3.7","title":"Build wrap-quality benchmark corpus and readability scorecard (greedy vs KP vs fallback)","description":"## Context\nSmart wrapping must improve readability without hidden regressions. We need a repeatable corpus and objective scorecard.\n\n## Scope\nCreate multilingual terminal-text corpora and scoring harnesses comparing greedy wrapping, KP wrapping, and fallback strategies.\n\n## Deliverables\n- Gold corpus with expected break quality notes\n- Automated scorer (badness/readability/consistency metrics)\n- Regression gate for wrap-quality deltas\n\n## Why this matters\nPrevents algorithm changes from silently degrading what users actually read.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Corpus covers code, logs, prose, long tokens, and Unicode-heavy samples.\n- Scorecard outputs machine-readable per-sample metrics and aggregate deltas.\n- CI/perf harness flags quality regressions beyond agreed tolerance.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:29.854499Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:51.581189Z","labels":["benchmark","knuth-plass","quality","reflow","resize","track-layout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.7","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:29.854499Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.7","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:38.570164Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-1u90p.3.7","depends_on_id":"wa-1u90p.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T00:52:38.672337Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-a4goc","title":"Phase 6: Build LabRuntime test infrastructure","description":"# Build LabRuntime test infrastructure

## Goal
Establish deterministic testing infrastructure using asupersync's LabRuntime. This is the foundation all tests are built on.

## LabRuntime capabilities to leverage
- **Virtual time**: Tests run instantly, no wall-clock deps
- **Deterministic scheduling**: Same seed = same execution order
- **Schedule exploration**: DPOR for concurrent interleaving coverage
- **Cancellation injection**: Test cancel-correctness at specific await points
- **Chaos testing**: Configurable failure injection (I/O errors, timeouts)
- **Trace capture/replay**: Reproduce failures exactly
- **Oracles**: DeterminismOracle, TaskLeakOracle, MailboxOracle, ObligationLeakOracle

## Deliverables

### Test helper module: tests/common/lab.rs
```rust
use asupersync::{LabRuntime, LabConfig, Cx, Outcome};
use tracing_subscriber::fmt;

/// Run a deterministic test with seed and structured logging
pub fn run_lab_test<F, Fut>(seed: u64, test_name: &str, f: F)
where
    F: FnOnce(&mut Cx) -> Fut,
    Fut: Future<Output = Outcome<(), TestError>>,
{
    // Set up structured logging (JSON format for machine parsing)
    let _guard = fmt::Subscriber::builder()
        .json()
        .with_test_writer()
        .with_target(true)
        .with_span_events(fmt::format::FmtSpan::FULL)
        .init();

    tracing::info!(seed, test_name, "Starting LabRuntime test");

    let lab = LabRuntime::new(LabConfig::default().seed(seed));
    let result = lab.run(|cx| f(cx));

    // Oracle checks
    assert!(lab.obligation_leak_oracle().is_ok(), "Obligation leak detected");
    assert!(lab.task_leak_oracle().is_ok(), "Task leak detected");

    tracing::info!(?result, "LabRuntime test completed");
    result.unwrap();
}

/// Run chaos test with failure injection
pub fn run_chaos_test<F, Fut>(seed: u64, chaos_config: ChaosConfig, f: F) { ... }

/// Run schedule exploration test (DPOR)
pub fn run_exploration_test<F, Fut>(max_schedules: usize, f: F) { ... }
```

### Structured logging conventions
All test helpers emit structured logs with:
- **seed**: LabRuntime seed for reproduction
- **test_name**: Human-readable test identifier
- **scope_id**: Which scope a task belongs to
- **channel_op**: reserve/commit/recv events with channel name
- **pool_stats**: Pool state snapshots (active, idle, total)
- **obligation_state**: Guard/permit creation and release events
- **virtual_time**: Current virtual timestamp

### Test fixture module: tests/common/fixtures.rs
- Mock UnixStream (loopback pair)
- Mock DirectMuxClient (returns canned PDUs)
- Mock Pool (configurable health check behavior)
- SimulatedNetwork for chaos testing

### Test categories (covered by module-specific test beads)
1. Connection pool: concurrent acquire/release, eviction, timeout
2. Mux client: PDU round-trip, subscription, cancellation
3. Channel: two-phase correctness, cancellation safety
4. Scope: structured concurrency guarantees
5. Cancellation: checkpoint behavior, multi-phase protocol
6. Chaos: random failure injection in I/O

## Acceptance criteria
- run_lab_test() helper with structured JSON logging
- run_chaos_test() helper with failure injection
- run_exploration_test() helper with DPOR
- Mock fixtures for UnixStream, Pool, DirectMuxClient
- Oracle assertions (obligation_leak, task_leak) in all helpers
- Logging captures seed, scope, channel ops, pool stats, virtual time
- All helpers compile and run with a basic smoke test

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for LabRuntime overhead vs real async**: Add `benches/labruntime_overhead.rs` measuring:
  - LabRuntime::run() setup + teardown overhead for an empty test
  - Virtual time cx.sleep() vs real async sleep resolution
  - DPOR exploration cost: time per schedule for a simple 2-task, 3-task, 5-task scenario
  - Oracle check overhead (obligation_leak_oracle, task_leak_oracle) per test
  - Comparison: LabRuntime test execution time vs equivalent #[tokio::test] for representative test cases

## Property-based testing
- **Proptest for schedule exhaustiveness**: Use proptest to generate arbitrary task graphs (random DAGs of spawn/join/channel operations) and verify that LabRuntime's DPOR exploration covers all linearizations. Compare DPOR-explored schedule count against theoretical minimum for the given task graph to ensure no interleavings are missed.

## Cross-references
- See wa-3kxe.4 (SPSC ring buffer testing with LabRuntime) — the SPSC ring buffer bead in the data structures epic uses LabRuntime for deterministic concurrency testing. Patterns and fixtures established here should be reusable for that bead's Loom/LabRuntime tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:51:00.595546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T01:54:57.003543-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T01:54:57.003543-05:00","close_reason":"All deliverables verified: tests/common/lab.rs (run_lab_test, run_chaos_test, run_exploration_test, run_multi_seed_test), tests/common/fixtures.rs (MockPool, MockMuxClient, MockUnixStream, SimulatedNetwork, RuntimeFixture, cancellation helpers), lab_smoke.rs (20 tests), labruntime_infrastructure.rs (20 tests), proptest_dpor_schedule.rs (4 tests), benches/labruntime_overhead.rs (12 criterion benchmarks). All 44 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-a4goc","depends_on_id":"wa-2lp7o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:52:02.781362Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-a4goc","depends_on_id":"wa-d0m4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:52:02.884615Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-aebw","title":"E2E script: command safety gate blocks destructive-looking sends (optional dcg)","description":"# Task: E2E test script — command safety gate

## Goal
Prove end-to-end that `wa` refuses (deny or require-approval) to inject destructive-looking commands, even when the target pane is prompt-active.

This validates PLAN.md §14.2 “Command Safety Gate”.

## Scenario
- Start mux server and watcher in a dedicated workspace.
- Spawn a dummy pane that is “safe to type into” (prompt-active markers) and echoes input.
- Run a set of sends that include:
  - safe prompt-like text (should be allowed)
  - safe command-like text (allowed)
  - destructive command-like text (deny or require-approval, depending on config)

Optional variant:
- With a stubbed `dcg` binary on PATH:
  - simulate dcg allow
  - simulate dcg deny

## Assertions
- A known destructive string (e.g., `rm -rf /`) is **not** injected:
  - robot/human output indicates Deny/RequireApproval with stable error codes
  - audit records the attempted action and the rule id/reason
  - pane output does not contain the destructive string

- A safe string *is* injected:
  - pane echoes it
  - audit records allow

- dcg integration behavior (if enabled in test config):
  - dcg deny produces RequireApproval/Deny (as configured)
  - dcg allow does not block

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging/artifacts
- Must follow the E2E harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and use structured logs (`wa-4vx.6.5`).
- Artifacts must include:
  - stdout/stderr of commands
  - watcher logs
  - audit export slice
  - a grep-style proof that destructive strings do not appear in pane tails or artifact files

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Include a control that proves the test would fail if the gate were disabled (e.g., flip config and assert injection occurs in that variant).
  - Explicitly scan *all* artifacts for the destructive string and fail if present.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The test is deterministic and CI-friendly.
- A regression in command safety gating fails loudly with artifacts that pinpoint:
  - classifier mistake
  - policy/rule evaluation mistake
  - dcg integration mistake

 {"id":"wa-am5","title":"E2E script: dry-run mode (wa send/workflow --dry-run previews)","description":"# E2E script: dry-run mode (previews for wa send / wa workflow / robot)

## Goal
Validate end-to-end that `--dry-run` produces accurate previews **without mutating anything**:
- correct target resolution (pane/domain/cwd/agent)
- correct policy evaluation (allow/deny/require-approval)
- correct “what would happen” step plan for workflows
- deterministic, schema-valid JSON output for machines

This is the integration proof for the dry-run epic (`wa-1pe`) using the shared E2E harness.

## Why this matters
Dry-run is the primary trust-building UX feature:
- users can explore safely
- robots can plan before acting
- reduces mistakes and support burden

## Key constraints
- **Zero side effects**:
  - must not send input to panes
  - must not acquire workflow locks
  - must not mark events handled
  - must not create workflow executions
  - may optionally write an audit entry tagged as a *dry-run evaluation* (explicitly non-action)
- **Deterministic**: no fixed sleeps; use bounded waits/quiescence.
- **Machine-stable**: tests should prefer `--format json` outputs (avoid scraping rich/TTY output).
- Uses the standard harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).

## Test setup
- Start a WezTerm mux server and spawn dummy panes that:
  - can echo any received input to prove whether a send occurred
  - can deterministically emit patterns to create events for workflow previews
- Run `wa watch` in an isolated E2E workspace.

## Scenarios

### 1) `wa send --dry-run` preview quality
- Run: `wa send --pane 3 --dry-run "hello" --format json`
- Assert preview includes:
  - resolved pane info (pane_id, title, cwd, agent)
  - policy decision + reasons (capabilities, safety gate)
  - an explicit “would send” description (bytes/line endings/control codes)
  - an explicit “would wait-for” plan if applicable

### 2) `wa send --dry-run` on a denied state
- Create a deterministic unsafe state (fixture / dummy alt-screen).
- Run: `wa send --pane 3 --dry-run "hello" --format json`
- Assert:
  - decision is deny/require-approval as expected
  - the preview explains remediation (e.g., exit AltScreen)
  - **no text was sent** (dummy pane echo confirms)

### 3) Preview vs actual: stable-field match
Goal: the preview’s *decision* and *target* must match the actual path.
- Capture preview JSON: `wa send … --dry-run --format json > artifacts/send_preview.json`
- Execute actual send: `wa send … --format json > artifacts/send_actual.json`
- Compare only stable fields:
  - `target.pane_id`
  - `policy.decision` + `policy.rule_id` (if present)
  - `action_kind`
  - (ignore timestamps, durations, correlation ids)

### 4) `wa workflow run --dry-run` step plan preview
- Ensure a deterministic unhandled event exists (dummy pane emits marker).
- Run: `wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane 3 --dry-run --format json`
- Assert preview includes:
  - step list in order (with step ids/names)
  - per-step policy checks (or a summarized policy gate)
  - expected waits/verifications
  - explicit note that no locks will be acquired and no DB state will change

### 5) Robot dry-run output (schema + no mutation)
- Run: `wa robot send --pane 3 --dry-run "hello" --format json`
- Run: `wa robot workflow handle_compaction 3 --dry-run --format json` (or the canonical robot workflow invocation)
- Assert outputs validate against the dry-run JSON schemas and include:
  - `decision` / `would_execute=false`
  - `steps_preview` (for workflows)

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`) with prerequisites and default inclusion status.

## Artifacts
- `send_preview.json`
- `send_actual.json`
- `workflow_preview.json`
- `dry_run_vs_actual.json` (comparison result)
- `wa_watch.log`
- `events.jsonl`
- `audit_slice.jsonl` (to prove “no action” vs “action”)

## Logging contract
Log per scenario:
- command invoked (redacted)
- timeout settings
- comparison summary (stable fields)

Example:
```
[DRYRUN_E2E] scenario=send_preview pane=3
[DRYRUN_E2E] decision=allow would_execute=false
[DRYRUN_E2E] scenario=preview_vs_actual stable_match=true
```

## Testing
- Determinism:
  - no fixed sleeps
  - every wait has a timeout and emits actionable failure output
- No-mutation proofs (pick at least two independent signals):
  - dummy pane did not echo received input
  - no new `send_text` audit action exists (only optional `dry_run` audit)
  - no `workflow_executions` row was created on dry-run
- Schema:
  - JSON outputs validate against the documented dry-run schema(s)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dry-run output is informative (target + policy + plan).
- [ ] Dry-run never mutates panes/locks/handled state.
- [ ] Preview decisions match actual decisions on stable fields.
- [ ] Robot dry-run output is schema-valid and deterministic.
- [ ] Artifacts/logging make failures easy to debug.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyLake","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:11:33.257051753Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-06T03:32:32.007499983Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T03:32:32.007423661Z","close_reason":"implemented","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-am5","depends_on_id":"wa-2vr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-am5","depends_on_id":"wa-mxzp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-am5","depends_on_id":"wa-1pe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-am5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-am5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-am5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-am5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-an1i","title":"Scheduler + alert execution for saved searches","description":"## What
Add a background scheduler to execute saved searches and emit alerts.

## Why
Scheduled alerts turn wa into a proactive monitor rather than a pull-only tool.

## How
- Integrate with watcher runtime loop or dedicated task
- Enforce max frequency + backoff
- Emit notification events with redacted snippets + counts

## Risks
- Alert storm risk; must rate-limit and include dedupe window

## Success Criteria
- Scheduled searches run on interval
- Alerts include query name, scope, and match count","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TopazStone","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:01:42.921020652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.289441-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-06T01:01:25.758761084Z","close_reason":"done","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-an1i","depends_on_id":"wa-uyve","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-an1i","depends_on_id":"wa-itft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-an1i","depends_on_id":"wa-4x5g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}
